channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Australian father of teen sextortion victim backs banning young children from social media
item.description: Wayne Holdsworth became an advocate for banning Australian children younger than 16 from social media because his son took his own life after falling victim to an online sextortion scam.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Worst November snowstorm in half century hits Seoul and grounds hundreds of airplane flights
item.description: The worst November snowstorm in half century has hit Seoul, grounding hundreds of lights countrywide and disrupting commuter traffic in the capital.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Middle East latest: Displaced people return to south Lebanon as ceasefire appears to hold
item.description: Long-displaced residents of south Lebanon started returning to their homes amid celebrations hours after a ceasefire between Israel and the Hezbollah militant group took effect early Wednesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Middle East latest: Ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon begins
item.description: A ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon’s Hezbollah militants began early Wednesday morning, after Beirut residents endured the most intense day of Israeli strikes since the war began. Many wondered if the agreement to stop fighting would hold.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel and Lebanon's Hezbollah start a ceasefire after nearly 14 months of fighting
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel and Lebanon-based Hezbollah militants began a ceasefire Wednesday in a major step toward ending nearly 14 months of fighting as a region on edge wondered whether it will hold.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN, KAREEM CHEHAYEB and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: An Australia police officer who shocked a 95-year-old woman with a Taser is guilty of manslaughter
item.description: A police officer in Australia who shocked a 95-year-old nursing home resident with a Taser was found guilty of manslaughter on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Childhood cancer patients in Lebanon must battle disease while under fire
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Carol Zeghayer gripped her IV as she hurried down the brightly lit hallway of Beirut’s children’s cancer center. The 9-year-old's face brightened when she spotted her playmates from the oncology ward.
item.dc:creator: SALLY ABOU AL JOUD, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Pakistani authorities start clearing Imran Khan supporters from the capital after deadly clashes
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Pakistani security forces launched an operation Tuesday night to disperse supporters of imprisoned former Prime Minister Imran Khan who had gathered in the capital to demand his release from prison.
item.dc:creator: RIAZAT BUTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What both sides are saying about the ceasefire deal between Israel and Lebanon's Hezbollah
item.description: A ceasefire deal that could end more than a year of cross-border fighting between Israel and Lebanon's Hezbollah militant group won backing from Israeli leaders Tuesday, raising hopes and renewing difficult questions in a region gripped by conflict.
item.dc:creator: ADAM GELLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Australia's House of Representatives passes bill that would ban young children from social media
item.description: Australia’s House of Representatives has passed a bill that would ban children younger than 16 years from social media.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A fugitive wanted in the US for a pair of bombings is arrested in the UK after 20 years on the run
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A suspected animal rights extremist wanted in the U.S. for bombings in the San Francisco area was arrested in Britain after more than 20 years on the run from the law, officials said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US responds to attack on troops with strike in Syria
item.description: U.S. forces on Tuesday struck Iranian-aligned militia groups in Syria in retaliation for the fighters attacking American bases. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Unidentified drones spotted over US military sites in UK for second week
item.description: The Pentagon is “actively” watching for drones over U.S. Air Force locations in the United Kingdom, following a series of unexplained sightings over the past two weeks. The sightings, which took place over three U.S. air bases last week between Nov.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Biden announces cease-fire plan between Israel and Hezbollah ending 14 months of fighting
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced his support for a cease-fire deal that has yet to be finalized between Israel and Hezbollah terrorists.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire to start Wednesday morning
item.description: The Israeli and U.S. governments announced a ceasefire agreement with Hezbollah in Lebanon that will begin Wednesday morning at 4 a.m. local time. “I have good news from the Middle East,” President Biden said in remarks from the White House Tuesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Blinken to appear at Afghanistan withdrawal hearing, top Republican says
item.description: Rep. Michael McCaul (R-Texas), the outgoing chair of the House Foreign Affairs Committee, said Secretary of State Antony Blinken is expected to testify before the committee about the Biden administration's withdrawal of troops from Afghanistan.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Bangladeshi court denies bail to Hindu leader who led rallies for minority protection
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — A prominent Bangladeshi Hindu leader who has been leading rallies demanding security for Hindus in the overwhelmingly Muslim nation was ordered detained on charges of sedition Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada braces for surge of immigrants hoping to move from US
item.description: Canadian officials will limit how many new permanent residents it allows amid an expected surge at the northern border.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, The Hill

item.title: Philippine president and vice president clash in a feud that's testing an Asian democracy
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — A major political storm set off by a bitter feud between the Philippine president and the vice president is testing an Asian democracy that has long been troubled by rowdy politics, poor law enforcement and bareface impunity.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rebel group takes key Myanmar border town and rare earth mining hub in setback for military regime
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A powerful rebel group has seized a key trading town in northeastern Myanmar on the Chinese border, taking control of a lucrative rare earth mining hub in another setback for the military-led government, according to witnesses.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean man convicted for deliberately gaining weight to evade military service
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A South Korean man has been sentenced to a suspended prison term for deliberately gaining more than 20 kilograms (44 pounds) to evade a tougher role in the country’s military conscription system, a Seoul court said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Amelia Earhart’s plane photo not of the plane, company says
item.description: “After 11 months the waiting has finally ended and unfortunately our target was not Amelia's Electra 10E," the exploration company said.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Israel 'moving forward' on possible Hezbollah cease-fire, official says
item.description: The Israeli Security Cabinet will convene Tuesday to discuss a possible cease-fire agreement with Hezbollah terrorists in Lebanon, Ambassador Danny Danon said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Uruguay ousts conservative government, elects leftist opposition candidate as turnout hits 90 percent
item.description: Leftist opposition candidate Yamandú Orsi claims victory in Uruguay's run-off presidential election, defeating center-right candidate Álvaro Delgado.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Egyptian officials say 16 people are missing after a tourist yacht sank in high waves on the Red Sea
item.description: Egyptian officials say a tourist yacht has sunk in the Red Sea after warnings of rough waters and 16 people are missing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Drones detected near 3 US bases in England last week: Air Force
item.description: Between Wednesday and Friday, multiple unidentified drones were detected near RAF Lakenheath, RAF Mildenhall and RAF Feltwell.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli strikes in central Beirut kill at least 20 as diplomats push for a cease-fire
item.description: BEIRUT, Lebanon (AP) — Israeli airstrikes Saturday in central Beirut killed at least 20 people, officials said, as the once-rare attacks on the heart of Lebanon's capital continued without warning while diplomats scrambled to broker a cease-fire.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The Philippine vice president publicly threatens to have the president assassinated
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte said Saturday she has contracted an assassin to kill the president, his wife and the House of Representatives speaker if she herself is killed, in a brazen public threat that she warned was not a joke.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Scientists study ‘very rare’ frozen remains of 35,000-year-old saber-toothed cub
item.description: Scientists studied a three-week-old saber-toothed cub dating back 35,000 that had been left almost perfectly intact, thanks to its frozen condition.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers make last-ditch effort to safeguard US support
item.description: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers in both parties are working to get key provisions surrounding U.S. support for Kyiv passed into law by January, an effort to safeguard assistance that is under threat in a transition to a Trump administration and full GOP control of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Catherine Herridge wants Trump 'sit-down' on bipartisan press bill
item.description: “This case involves the First Amendment and the right of reporters in the future,” Catherine Herridge said.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Death toll in Gaza from Israel-Hamas war passes 44,000, Palestinian officials say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — The death toll in the Gaza Strip from the 13-month-old war between Israel and Hamas has surpassed 44,000, local health officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Admiral: Middle East, Ukraine conflicts strain US air defense stocks
item.description: Conflicts in the Middle East and Ukraine are adding a strain to the U.S. military’s ability to respond to China, a top U.S. admiral warns.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: US vetoes UN resolution demanding a cease-fire in Gaza because there's no link to a hostage release
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United States on Wednesday vetoed a U.N. Security Council resolution demanding an immediate cease-fire in the war in Gaza because it was not linked to an immediate release of hostages taken captive by Hamas militants in Israel in October 2023.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, WGN 9
